qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: StackGlobal opcode enables dynamic module name construction bypassing static import analysis
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about fickling's StackGlobal.run() (fickling/fickle.py:1119-1171) not validating dynamically constructed module names, enabling bypass of the UNSAFE_MODULES blocklist through the STACK_GLOBAL pickle opcode. The advisory root cause is the specific omission of cProfile from the UNSAFE_MODULES blocklist in fickling/analysis.py. These are different vulnerable code locations and different root causes: the advisory requires adding cProfile to a static list, while this finding requires tracking dynamic name construction in the StackGlobal handler. The finding does not identify or reference cProfile at all.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-p523-jq9w-64x9) describes cProfile missing from the UNSAFE_MODULES blocklist in fickling/analysis.py, class UnsafeImports. The only qualifying finding, 'stack_global_dynamic_import_bypass', is about fickling but addresses a different root cause: the STACK_GLOBAL opcode enabling dynamic module name construction that evades static analysis, not a specific blocklist omission of cProfile. The non_qualifying_vuln finding 'incomplete_unsafe_modules_blocklist' describes the same incomplete-blocklist pattern but focuses on different missing modules (multiprocessing, threading, ctypes, etc.) and does not mention cProfile; it is also non-qualifying and cannot be selected.
evidence: Advisory identifies root cause as cProfile missing from UNSAFE_MODULES in fickling/analysis.py, fixed by commit dc8ae12966edee27a78fe05c5745171a2b138d43.

The 'stack_global_dynamic_import_bypass' finding targets fickling/fickle.py:1119-1171 (StackGlobal.run()) which is a different vulnerable code location than fickling/analysis.py UnsafeImports blocklist.
The 'stack_global_dynamic_import_bypass' finding does not mention cProfile, cProfile.run, or cProfile.runctx anywhere in its report or PoC.
